title: ISO 27001 Annex A Controls Quick Reference
content:
# ISO 27001 Annex A Controls Quick Reference

## Overview

ISO/IEC 27001:2022 is the international standard for information security management systems (ISMS). It specifies requirements for establishing, implementing, maintaining, and continually improving an ISMS. The 2022 revision reorganized controls from 14 categories (114 controls) to 4 themes (93 controls), making the framework more modern and practical.

ISO 27001 certification requires an accredited third-party audit and is recognized globally. Unlike SOC 2 which is a report, ISO 27001 results in a certificate valid for three years with annual surveillance audits.

## Requirements

### ISO 27001:2022 Annex A Control Themes

The 93 controls are organized into four themes:

| Theme | Controls | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| A.5 Organizational Controls | 37 controls | Policies, roles, responsibilities, threat intelligence, asset management, access |
| A.6 People Controls | 8 controls | Screening, terms of employment, awareness, disciplinary process, remote working |
| A.7 Physical Controls | 14 controls | Physical perimeters, entry controls, securing offices, equipment, cabling |
| A.8 Technological Controls | 34 controls | User endpoints, privileged access, access restriction, secure coding, monitoring |

### Key Organizational Controls (A.5)

| Control | Title | SOC Focus |
|---------|-------|----------|
| A.5.1 | Policies for information security | Foundation — all other controls reference policies |
| A.5.2 | Information security roles and responsibilities | Defines SOC analyst roles and escalation paths |
| A.5.7 | Threat intelligence | Directly relevant — CTI integration, feed management |
| A.5.8 | Information security in project management | Security review in new system deployments |
| A.5.23 | Information security for use of cloud services | Cloud security controls, shared responsibility |
| A.5.24 | Information security incident management planning | IR plan documentation and maintenance |
| A.5.25 | Assessment and decision on information security events | Event triage and incident classification |
| A.5.26 | Response to information security incidents | Incident response execution |
| A.5.27 | Learning from information security incidents | Post-incident review, lessons learned |
| A.5.28 | Collection of evidence | Digital forensics, chain of custody |
| A.5.29 | Information security during disruption | Business continuity, DR procedures |
| A.5.30 | ICT readiness for business continuity | System resilience, failover capabilities |

### Key Technological Controls (A.8)

| Control | Title | Implementation |
|---------|-------|--------------|
| A.8.1 | User endpoint devices | EDR/XDR deployment, device management, disk encryption |
| A.8.2 | Privileged access rights | PAM solution, just-in-time access, session recording |
| A.8.5 | Secure authentication | MFA, password policies, SSO integration |
| A.8.9 | Configuration management | Hardened baselines, CIS benchmarks, drift detection |
| A.8.12 | Data leakage prevention | DLP policies, email scanning, endpoint DLP |
| A.8.15 | Logging | Centralized logging, SIEM integration, log integrity |
| A.8.16 | Monitoring activities | SIEM alerting, SOC operations, anomaly detection |
| A.8.23 | Web filtering | URL filtering, category blocking, SSL inspection |
| A.8.25 | Secure development lifecycle | SAST, DAST, SCA, security code review |
| A.8.28 | Secure coding | OWASP guidelines, input validation, output encoding |

### New Controls in ISO 27001:2022

The 2022 revision introduced 11 new controls not present in the 2013 version:

| Control | Title | Why Added |
|---------|-------|----------|
| A.5.7 | Threat intelligence | Formalize CTI programs |
| A.5.23 | Cloud services security | Address cloud adoption |
| A.5.30 | ICT readiness for business continuity | Strengthen BC/DR |
| A.7.4 | Physical security monitoring | Surveillance, physical detection |
| A.8.9 | Configuration management | Formalize configuration baselines |
| A.8.10 | Information deletion | Data lifecycle, right to erasure |
| A.8.11 | Data masking | Anonymization, pseudonymization |
| A.8.12 | Data leakage prevention | DLP controls |
| A.8.16 | Monitoring activities | SOC/SIEM operations |
| A.8.23 | Web filtering | URL/content filtering |
| A.8.28 | Secure coding | Secure development practices |

## Implementation Guidance

### ISMS Implementation Steps

**Phase 1: Establish (2-3 months)**
1. Define ISMS scope (which business units, locations, systems)
2. Develop information security policy (signed by top management)
3. Conduct risk assessment using ISO 27005 or similar methodology
4. Select applicable Annex A controls based on risk assessment
5. Develop Statement of Applicability (SoA) — lists all 93 controls with justification for inclusion/exclusion
6. Develop risk treatment plan with control implementation timeline

**Phase 2: Implement (3-6 months)**
1. Implement selected controls per risk treatment plan
2. Develop supporting policies, procedures, and work instructions
3. Deploy technical controls (SIEM, EDR, DLP, PAM, etc.)
4. Conduct security awareness training for all employees
5. Establish internal audit program
6. Implement document control and records management

**Phase 3: Operate (3-6 months minimum before certification)**
1. Run the ISMS — collect evidence of control operation
2. Conduct internal audits covering all clauses and applicable controls
3. Perform management review (at least annually, documented)
4. Monitor and measure ISMS performance (KPIs, metrics)
5. Manage nonconformities and corrective actions

**Phase 4: Certify**
1. **Stage 1 audit**: Auditor reviews documentation (ISMS design)
2. **Stage 2 audit**: Auditor tests control effectiveness (on-site or remote)
3. Address any nonconformities raised
4. Receive certificate (valid 3 years)
5. Annual surveillance audits (years 2 and 3)
6. Recertification audit (year 3)

### SOC Team's Role in ISO 27001

The SOC directly supports multiple Annex A controls:

| SOC Activity | Annex A Controls Supported |
|-------------|---------------------------|
| Alert monitoring and triage | A.8.16, A.5.25 |
| Incident response | A.5.24, A.5.26, A.5.27 |
| Threat intelligence | A.5.7 |
| Log management | A.8.15 |
| Vulnerability management | A.8.8 |
| Evidence collection (forensics) | A.5.28 |
| Security reporting | A.5.25, A.8.16 |

## Evidence Collection

| Control Area | Evidence Required |
|-------------|------------------|
| Policies (A.5.1) | Approved policy documents, version history, acknowledgment records |
| Access Control (A.5.15-A.5.18, A.8.2-A.8.5) | Access request forms, RBAC matrix, access review records, MFA configuration |
| Logging & Monitoring (A.8.15, A.8.16) | SIEM configuration, alert rules, sample incident tickets, log retention policy |
| Incident Management (A.5.24-A.5.28) | IR plan, incident log, post-incident reports, tabletop exercise records |
| Risk Assessment (A.5.1-A.5.6) | Risk assessment methodology, risk register, treatment plans, management approval |
| Business Continuity (A.5.29, A.5.30) | BCP/DR plans, test results, RTO/RPO documentation |
| Training (A.6.3) | Training materials, completion records, phishing simulation results |

## Common Findings

1. **Statement of Applicability incomplete or outdated**: SoA must list all 93 controls with clear justification. Update after every risk assessment.

2. **Risk assessment not linked to controls**: Controls selected without documented risk justification. Each control in the SoA should trace to identified risks.

3. **Internal audit coverage gaps**: Not all clauses and controls audited within the cycle. Create a three-year internal audit plan covering everything.

4. **Management review insufficient**: Review is perfunctory or missing required inputs (audit results, risk status, metrics, improvement opportunities). Follow clause 9.3 requirements exactly.

5. **Corrective actions not effective**: Nonconformities addressed superficially without root cause analysis. Implement proper root cause analysis and verify effectiveness.

6. **Monitoring and measurement undefined**: No KPIs or metrics for ISMS performance. Define measurable objectives (e.g., MTTD, MTTR, patch SLA compliance).

7. **Document control weaknesses**: Policies and procedures without version control, approval, or review dates. Implement document management system with review cycles.

8. **Cloud services not adequately addressed**: A.5.23 is new in 2022 — many organizations have not formally documented cloud security controls, shared responsibility models, or cloud-specific risk assessments.

## References

- [ISO/IEC 27001:2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001)
- [ISO/IEC 27002:2022 Implementation Guidance](https://www.iso.org/standard/75652.html)
- [ISO/IEC 27005:2022 Risk Management](https://www.iso.org/standard/80585.html)
